input.initialPrompt: Before creating anything, show me exactly what files would be generated if I create a Blazor Web App called MyBlazorApp in ./test-output with interactive server rendering. Don't actually create the project yet.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to see what files would be generated by creating a Blazor Web App with interactive server rendering, without actually creating the project. Let me use the template-engine custom agent to handle this, as it's specialized for dotnet new operations.

Actually, let me think about this. The user wants a "dry run" preview of what files a `dotnet new blazor` (or similar) template would produce. I can use the template-discovery skill or template-instantiation skill to inspect what the template produces. Let me use the template-engine agent since it can route to the right skill.

Actually, the simplest approach is to use `dotnet new blazor --dry-run` which shows what files would be created without actually creating them. Let me just do that directly with bash.

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run 2>&1", "description": "Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity", "initial_wait": 15}

toolArgs.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

data.content:
Here's the dry-run output. The template would generate **68 files** across these groups:

| Directory | Files |
|---|---|
| `Components/` | `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `_Imports.razor` |
| `Components/Layout/` | `MainLayout`, `NavMenu`, `ReconnectModal` (`.razor`, `.razor.css`, some `.razor.js`) |
| `Components/Pages/` | `Counter`, `Error`, `Home`, `NotFound`, `Weather` (all `.razor`) |
| Root | `MyBlazorApp.csproj`, `Program.cs` |
| `Properties/` | `launchSettings.json` |
| Config | `appsettings.json`, `appsettings.Development.json` |
| `wwwroot/` | `app.css`, `favicon.png` |
| `wwwroot/lib/bootstrap/` | Full Bootstrap CSS + JS dist (~46 files) |

A post-creation **NuGet restore** would also run automatically. Say the word if you'd like me to create it for real.
